priceSnapshot.source: Yahoo Finance (Catherine Brock, 11:22 a.m. ET) + intraday aggregators (Coinpedia/CoinDesk)
priceSnapshot.note: BTC lost the multi-session $64,062 range floor on the US-morning risk-off wave: opened $65,047.87, printed $64,304.50 by 9:13 a.m. ET and fell to an intraday low of $63,713 before steadying near ~$63,968 (down ~1.66% versus the ~$65,048 open). The morning invalidation/reduce level at $64,062 has been breached — the range that framed the prior three sessions has broken to the downside.

reduceIf.confirmation: 1H close below $63,500 loses the OTE/higher-low zone — scale the remaining core toward EXIT with the next shelf $62,700 then $62,000

addIf.confirmation: 1H close back above $64,300 (ideally reclaim $64,800) recaptures the lost range floor — only then re-add the trimmed tranche

decisionBox.changed: The morning HOLD thesis was invalidated intraday: BTC broke the $64,062 range floor that had held for three sessions, tagging $63,713 as $100 Brent + a 4.71% 10Y pulled institutions out of risk. The morning REDUCE/invalidation trigger FIRED, so the stance shifts HOLD->REDUCE. Losing $63,500 next opens the $62,700/$62,000 shelf; a reclaim of $64,300+ would call the break a stop-run and restore the range.

atr.source: Not verified this run (exchange APIs blocked from sandbox). Prior verified daily ATR(14) ~$1,553 (~2.36%) on Jul 22 — deliberately NOT carried into any calculation here; the ~$1,987 24h high-low range ($63,713-$65,700) is consistent with an expanded, above-average ATR day.

timeframes.structure: Impulsive break of $64,062 into $63,713 then a shallow bounce to ~$63,968; lower-highs/lower-lows intraday. Sellers in control while capped under $64,300.
timeframes.source: Price action per Yahoo Finance / Coinpedia (Jul 24); intraday indicator table not verified (exchange APIs blocked)

timeframes.structure: Lost the $64,800-$65,000 demand and then the $64,062 floor; the broken floor now caps as first resistance. Structure flips to range-breakdown unless $64,300+ is reclaimed on a close.

First 4H close below the multi-session $64,062 range low ends the compression to the downside; $63,500 is the OTE/higher-low pivot that must hold to keep the July higher-low sequence ($62,000/$62,700/$63,500) intact.
timeframes.source: Structure per Yahoo Finance / Coinpedia (Jul 24); indicators not verified

timeframes.structure: Still a higher-low July repair for now (price ~$63,968 remains above the ~$63,000 50-day SMA), but today's floor break is the first real crack; the daily EMA200 continues to sit well above price so the higher-TF downtrend is intact. Daily RSI not re-verified this run.
timeframes.source: Structure per Yahoo Finance / Coinpedia (Jul 24); daily RSI/EMAs not re-verified this run

scalpContext.note: Scalp only the edges — live signals come from the chart, not this report.

etfFlows.note: No fresh Jul 24 flow print yet (T+1 reporting). The seven-day US spot BTC ETF inflow streak (~$999M cumulative per Coinglass) SNAPPED on Jul 23 with ~$225.2M of net outflows led by IBIT; the structural bid remains absent, which is the backdrop for today's floor break.

liquidations.note: The flush from $64,300 to $63,713 almost certainly triggered long liquidations, but a clean, current long/short USD split was not verifiable this run and is left null rather than fabricated.

earnings.note: Jul 23 post-close Q2 beat (rev $16.13B, +25% YoY; adj EPS $0.42) spiked shares toward $110 then gave most of it back; a fading, now-stale positive that did not offset the macro risk-off into Jul 24.
Jul 23 post-earnings drop on the lifted 2026 capex guide ($195-205B); still weighing on Nasdaq sentiment into Friday.
Jul 23 miss (rev $22.5B, -12% YoY; EPS $0.40); a lingering drag on megacap/risk appetite.

indices.note: Last close (Jul 23) -1.90% at $691.96 as $100 oil and rising yields hammered tech — the lowest close of the trailing 21 sessions. Jul 24 session in progress; DCA computed on trailing daily closes through Jul 23.

dcaSignal.note: Computed from trailing daily closes — relative-cheapness for monthly DCA, not a bottom call.

indices.note: Last close (Jul 23) -1.23% at $678.61 on the oil/yields risk-off; sits mid its trailing-monthly range. Jul 24 session in progress; DCA computed on trailing daily closes through Jul 23.

dcaSignal.note: Computed from trailing daily closes — relative-cheapness for monthly DCA, not a bottom call.

indices.note: StockAnalysis feed remains STALE — last print still dated Jun 18, 2026, not a current session. Per the data-integrity rule, price/changePct are null and dcaSignal is omitted.

calibration.rollingRecord: The range-with-bearish-tilt sequence paid off: after several sessions of correctly flagging $64,062 as the line in the sand, the floor finally broke intraday and the resting stop/REDUCE plan did its job.
calibration.note: The morning REDUCE/invalidation at $64,062 FIRED — BTC traded through it to a $63,713 low and holds ~$63,968. A spot holder who kept the resting stop at $64,062 (or trimmed on the 1H break) avoided riding the drop lower, so acting helped. Bias steps from range/bearish-tilt to outright bearish (bear 39->48, range 40->37, bull 21->15) until $64,300 is reclaimed.
